characters.name: Bank Manager
characters.description: Bank ManagerBiographical informationMarital statusMarriedPhysical descriptionGenderMaleOther informationRelativesWife (currently estranged)Series informationPortrayed byHugh DennisFirst episodeEpisode 1.1Last episodeEpisode 2.5Bank Manageris a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseries of the comedy dramaFleabag. He is played byHugh Dennis. He is currently in the middle of a sexual harassment scandal which has caused him to separate from his wife. Devastated by his mistake, he is trying to make amends. He is technically the only other character in the show, besides Fleabag herself, to also appear in the original stage play: his voice is heard in both the opening and closing scenes of the play, set during Fleabag's interview for a loan.

characters.name: Boo
characters.description: BooBiographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderFemaleEye colorBlueSeries informationPortrayed byJenny RainsfordFirst episodeEpisode 1.1Last episodeEpisode 2.4Boois a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseries of the comedy dramaFleabag. She wasFleabag's best friend, and co-owner of the cafe that the two of them ran. She is played byJenny Rainsford. Boo accidentally killed herself after learning that her boyfriend had cheated on her. She intended to injure herself by stepping out into a bike lane, but the resulting crash caused the death of three people, including Boo.

characters.name: Claire
characters.description: ClaireBiographical informationMarital statusDivorcedPhysical descriptionGenderFemaleOther informationRelativesDad(father)Godmother(step-mother)Fleabag(sister)Martin(husband)Jake (step-son)Series informationPortrayed bySian CliffordFirst episodeEpisode 1.1Last episodeEpisode 2.6Claireis a main character in thefirstandsecondseries of the comedy dramaFleabag. She isFleabag's sister. She was married toMartin, and was step-mother to his son, Jake. She has a strained relationship with her sister Fleabag, but obviously really loves her. She is played bySian Clifford. Claire has been described as tasteful and possibly anorexic because of her great outfits and fit figure. According to Fleabag she also got all of their mom’s good bits. She initially had a traditional shoulder length hairstyle but mid-season 2, had a short French style uneven bob that actually suited her quite well. She is mostly seen in muted colored clothes and sensible and stylish shoes except when she goes through anything that stresses her out which prompts her to opt for eclectic articles of clothing or footwear. She doesn’t wear heavy makeup and goes for a more natural look in the nude palette. Claire reunites with Fleabag for their family dinner where their father announces he’s engaged to their godmother. It's a very tense encounter, where she mostly ignores Fleabag, until she rushes to the bathroom where she reveals to Fleabag that she is having a miscarriage. She initially decides not to go to the hospital, but Fleabag causes a scene which ends the dinner party, and they go to the hospital together.

characters.name: Fleabag
characters.description: FleabagBiographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderFemaleEye colorBrownOther informationRelativesDadGodmotherClaireSeries informationPortrayed byPhoebe Waller-BridgeFirst episodeEpisode 1.1Last episodeEpisode 2.6Fleabagis a thirty-three year old single woman and the main character of the eponymousseries. She is played byPhoebe Waller-Bridge. Fleabag jokes darkly that she is "motherless", having lost her mother to cancer three years before the series begins. The series frequently explores her relationships with her remaining family (specifically her father and sister), as well as her grief and guilt over the recent death of her best friend. Fleabag struggles with intimacy issues, which manifests in an addiction to sex and masturbation. Fleabag is relentlessly snarky and sassy. She can't help but crack a joke, even in the most inappropriate moments. She has a lot of sexual encounters, although few with real meaning. She is interested in men most of the time, although occasionally women. Fleabag is open minded and generally up for whatever and whomever. Fleabag is relentlessly snarky and sassy. She can't help but crack a joke, even in the most inappropriate moments. She has a lot of sexual encounters, although few with real meaning. She is interested in men most of the time, although occasionally women. Fleabag is open minded and generally up for whatever and whomever. Deep down, Fleabag has a great heart. She is always there for her sister and tries to protect her, even when Claire resists.

characters.name: The Priest
characters.description: The PriestBiographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderMaleEye colorBrownSeries informationPortrayed byAndrew ScottFirst episodeEpisode 2.1Most recent episodeEpisode 2.6The Priestis a main character in thesecondseries ofFleabag. He is portrayed byAndrew Scott. He is charismatic with a blunt personality. He often swears and jokes - unlike the "typical priest". He likes helping people with their troubles, even to the point that it comes as a default. Despite being charming, he claims he has no friends, and that he spends his time reading. The Priest is an attractive man, in his late thirties or early forties, with dark brown hair. He is charismatic with a blunt personality. He often swears and jokes - unlike the "typical priest". He likes helping people with their troubles, even to the point that it comes as a default. Despite being charming, he claims he has no friends, and that he spends his time reading.
